Operation '/GetReport' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is generic and doesn't clarify the action. Description is developer-focused and unclear about response behavior for AI agents. Improved summary uses action-oriented language. Enhanced description clarifies: (1) the three ProductID options and their meanings, (2) immediate vs. asynchronous response behavior, (3) the AppID return value and its purpose, (4) the workflow dependency with GetArchiveReport, and (5) processing timeframes. This enables AI agents to understand when to use this operation and how to handle different response scenarios.

data.current_summary: Get report associated with criminal record history on an individual
data.current_description:
When requesting the US Criminal Report, users must specify the ProductID tag with the value that indicates the report option desired. Users should populate one of the below options for each request:

* US Criminal Database Report……Specify ProductID "USCRMD"
* US Criminal County Report………Specify ProductID "USCRMC"
* US Criminal Statewide Report…...Specify ProductID "USCRMS"

IMPORTANT DEVELOPER NOTE:

When testing the "USCRMC" and "USCRMS" products, these are direct/manual searches of County and Statewide court records. Therefore, the GetReport response for these products will always deliver an "IN_PROC" Status to indicate the results are not instant and require a secondary API call. To retrieve the final results for the USCRMC and USCRMS products, users must capture the AppID from the initial GetReport call, and submit the AppID to the GetArchiveReport API to receive final results.
 

For testing purposes, the test URL and sandbox for GetArchiveReport will deliver results from captured AppIDs without delay. However, in the Production environment, users are recommended to check the GetArchiveReport API periodically to determine if results are available.  Some Statewide reports may return more quickly than County reports due to automated direct connections available from some States, while County searches require court researchers to manually visit courts on-site, which can take anywhere from 3-7 days on average.
data.summary_suggestion: Submit criminal report request for individual background check
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a criminal background report search for an individual. Requires specifying a ProductID to select the report type: USCRMD (national database), USCRMC (county court records), or USCRMS (statewide records). Returns an AppID and Status. For database reports (USCRMD), results are returned immediately. For county and statewide reports (USCRMC, USCRMS), the response returns Status "IN_PROC" indicating asynchronous processing; use the returned AppID with GetArchiveReport to retrieve final results (typically 3-7 days for county searches, faster for statewide depending on state automation). Results are cached for 90 days.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic and doesn't clarify the action. Description is developer-focused and unclear about response behavior for AI agents. Improved summary uses action-oriented language. Enhanced description clarifies: (1) the three ProductID options and their meanings, (2) immediate vs. asynchronous response behavior, (3) the AppID return value and its purpose, (4) the workflow dependency with GetArchiveReport, and (5) processing timeframes. This enables AI agents to understand when to use this operation and how to handle different response scenarios.

message: Operation '/GetArchiveReport' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary uses unclear terminology (RqUID/AppId) and doesn't explain the operation's purpose. Description is too brief and lacks context about when/why to use this operation. Improved summary clarifies the action and key parameter. Enhanced description explains: (1) the relationship to GetReport, (2) when this operation is needed (asynchronous reports), (3) the required AppID parameter, (4) what data is returned, (5) the 90-day cache window, and (6) expected processing timeframes. This helps AI agents understand this is a follow-up operation for retrieving asynchronous results.

data.current_summary: Request previously obtained report by using its RqUID (AppId).
data.current_description: Previously pulled reports are available on the system for immediate access for up to 90 days.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve completed criminal report results by AppID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the final results of a previously submitted criminal report request using the AppID returned from GetReport. This operation is required for asynchronous report types (USCRMC county and USCRMS statewide searches) to obtain results after processing completes. Accepts AppID as a required parameter. Returns the complete criminal report data including felony convictions, misdemeanors, arrest records, sex offender registry information, incarceration records, and traffic violations (up to 7 years history where applicable). Cached results are available for 90 days from the initial request. Poll this endpoint periodically for county searches (3-7 days typical) or statewide searches (varies by state automation).
